1117.reason: [The condition `assign > curcount` is incorrect. When `assign` is found, it is set to the index of the ASSIGN token, which is always greater than `curcount` (the current position). However, if no ASSIGN is found on the same line, `assign` remains 0, and `0 > curcount` is false only when `curcount` is 0, which is not the intended logic. The correct condition should be `assign != 0` to check if an ASSIGN was found.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition `assign > curcount` is incorrect. When `assign` is found, it is set to the index of the ASSIGN token, which is always greater than `curcount` (the current position). However, if no ASSIGN is found on the same line, `assign` remains 0, and `0 > curcount` is false only when `curcount` is 0, which is not the intended logic. The correct condition should be `assign != 0` to check if an ASSIGN was found.]
